description: Create a two-column content slide on data and AI with an executive quote and commentary on the left and four recommended actions in a right-hand bullet list.

left.title: Paul Henninger, Head of Connected Technology, KPMG in the UK
left.body:
“Strong data governance treats data as a set of products with one or more specific business goals, and all maintenance, storage, and quality standards are oriented around that on an ongoing basis. Some leading banks and insurers have started to adopt that approach, and it is paying off for them because it moves away from the idea that data needs to be perfect before it can be useful.”

To transform the value chain end-to-end, businesses will need to continually address data siloes by strengthening data governance and integration, and consider how to bridge a skills gap.

Companies across financial services are currently experiencing a bottleneck as they look to scale their AI adoption. A recent KPMG survey found that only 16 percent of organizations have a workforce that is well equipped to implement GenAI, with 61 percent looking to hire new talent in this area. A multifaceted approach is proving to be a critical strategy adopted by digital leaders, including partnering with third parties to expand the talent pool, providing learning programs and global delivery centers to upskill and engage with the workforce, buying off-the-shelf assets and solutions, and co-investing with start-ups or fintech companies to leverage their speed, skills, and balance sheet.

right.title: The financial services sector should consider these challenges with urgency. We recommend the following actions, derived from analysis on the performance of global leaders:

items.sub: Legacy systems are holding AI back. Invest to upgrade, replatform or consider low-code options to help augment legacy systems where they are impeding the use of AI.
Establish the data foundations needed to enable your vision, including data platforms, products, and governance. Build confidence in the quality of data to improve outcomes from the use of AI.

Identify and plan for your future AI talent. Explore the requirements for upskilling your current team while identifying future sources of skills including partnering or co-investing with start-ups or tech companies.
Redesign operating models to embed AI effectively across the enterprise and enable scalable adoption.

footer.source: KPMG LLP, “KPMG Survey: GenAI Dramatically Shifting How Leaders Are Charting the Course for Their Organizations” (August 15, 2024).
